skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks with ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. ElevenLabs Music turns a style description plus structured lyrics into studio-quality 44.1 kHz stereo audio — 5 seconds to 5 minutes — with section-level control (Intro / Verse / Chorus / Bridge), multilingual vocals, and commercial-friendly output. Generate a backing track, a full vocal song, a jingle, a podcast intro, a game loop, or an instrumental bed. Calls `runcomfy run elevenlabs/elevenlabs/music-generation` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "generate music", "make a song", "AI music", "background music", "instrumental track", "ElevenLabs Music", "soundtrack", "jingle", "theme music", "royalty-free music", "compose", or any explicit ask to generate music or a song from a text description.
skill.summary: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks with ElevenLabs Music AI via the runcomfy CLI
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 93 potential issues, but evaluation reveals all external_commands patterns are markdown code formatting (backticks for inline code), weak_crypto detections are numeric strings (music durations/frequencies not crypto), and network URLs are legitimate RunComfy documentation links. The skill invokes the runcomfy CLI to call the ElevenLabs Music API - a standard music generation wrapper with no malicious behavior detected. All shell commands are pre-defined CLI invocations; no user input injection vectors exist.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 69 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. Evaluation shows these are Markdown code formatting syntax used throughout documentation, not executable shell commands. The backticks surround CLI examples like `runcomfy run elevenlabs/...` for documentation display.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick instances in SKILL.md are clearly markdown inline code formatting for documenting CLI commands. No actual shell execution occurs from these patterns.

medium_findings.title: Numeric Values Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithms
medium_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 10 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. Evaluation reveals these are numeric strings representing music duration, frequency, and bar counts (e.g., '44.1 kHz', '5 seconds', '8 bars', '120 BPM'). No cryptographic algorithms are used in this skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching flagged numeric strings as crypto algorithms. No crypto usage exists in this skill - it only calls the runcomfy CLI to invoke the ElevenLabs Music API.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL References for Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to RunComfy documentation pages. These are necessary documentation links for a CLI wrapper skill. URLs target legitimate runcomfy.com domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate RunComfy documentation links. No third-party or untrusted endpoints present.

low_findings.title: Token Storage in User Home Directory
low_findings.description: The skill documents storing API tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This is standard CLI token storage behavior, documented with appropriate file permissions (mode 0600).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard token storage pattern for CLI tools. File permissions are properly documented, and the skill does not expose the token value.

content.user_title: Generate AI Music with ElevenLabs via RunComfy CLI
content.value_statement: Create studio-quality songs and instrumental tracks from text descriptions without manual music production expertise. ElevenLabs Music generates 44.1kHz stereo audio with section-level control using structured lyrics and style prompts.
actual_capabilities: Generate full vocal songs with verse/chorus/bridge structure from style brief plus lyrics
Create instrumental tracks and background music beds with force_instrumental mode
Produce 5-second to 5-minute audio at 44.1kHz stereo quality
Support multilingual vocals by writing lyrics in target language
Generate brand jingles, podcast intros, and game audio loops
Download generated audio directly via CLI --output-dir flag
limitations: Single prompt field carries both style description and lyrics - no separate lyric parameter
Maximum 5-minute duration per API call - longer pieces require external stitching
Output is commercial-friendly but depends on user-supplied lyrics rights
Cannot request specific vocal identities or voice cloning

use_cases.title: Content Creator Video Production
use_cases.description: Generate a custom theme song or background music track for YouTube videos, podcasts, or streaming content. Specify the mood, tempo, and duration to match your video length exactly.

use_cases.title: Indie Game Developer Audio
use_cases.description: Create seamless background loops and game audio without hiring a composer. Generate loop-friendly instrumental tracks that match your game genre and atmosphere.
use_cases.target_user: Indie game developers and small studios

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Brand Assets
use_cases.description: Produce professional jingles, stingers, and audio brand assets for ads, intros, and promotional materials without music production skills.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Instrumental Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a calm lo-fi hip-hop instrumental for a study playlist. Warm Rhodes piano, soft vinyl crackle, mellow boom-bap drums, 75 BPM. No vocals. 60 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need simple background music without vocals

prompt_templates.title: Full Vocal Song with Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an upbeat indie-pop anthem, bright electric guitars, driving drums, 120 BPM, female lead vocal. [Intro 8 bars] instrumental build. [Verse] Lyrics here. [Chorus] Full lyrics here. [Bridge] Soft breakdown. [Outro] Full band fade. 90 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a complete song with vocals and section structure

prompt_templates.title: Short Brand Jingle
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second cheerful brand stinger with bright marimba and single uplifting chord resolve. No vocals. Clean ending.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick brand audio asset

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Draft then Final
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft first: Generate a cinematic orchestral piece, epic dramatic mood, 80 BPM. [Intro] [Build] [Climax] [Resolution] 30 seconds. If direction is correct, generate full 3-minute version with extended [Climax] section.
prompt_templates.scenario: When validating music direction before committing to full render

output_examples.input: Generate a relaxing ambient track for a meditation app. Soft pads, natural water sounds, no vocals. 2 minutes.
output_examples.output: Studio-quality ambient audio file saved to output directory. 44.1kHz stereo MP3, 2 minutes duration, seamless loop-friendly structure.

output_examples.input: Create an upbeat corporate jingle, positive and energetic. Include vocals singing 'Your Brand Name'. 15 seconds.
output_examples.output: Professional jingle audio file with upbeat tempo and commercial-friendly vocals, ready for marketing use.

output_examples.input: Generate game background music: epic fantasy battle theme, driving percussion, orchestral strings, no vocals. 60 seconds.
output_examples.output: Epic instrumental track with cinematic feel, high-energy percussion and sweeping strings for action sequences.

best_practices: Lead with the music style brief including genre, mood, tempo (BPM), and key instruments before lyrics
Draft short (30-45 seconds) to validate direction before paying for full-length renders
Use section markers [Intro], [Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro] with estimated bar counts for structure
anti_patterns: Avoid contradictory style instructions like 'aggressive metal' combined with 'soft lullaby' in one prompt
Do not expect specific vocal identities or reproduce particular singers - this endpoint does not support voice cloning
Refrain from using copyrighted lyrics - confirm you have rights to any lyrics submitted

faq.question: How long can the generated music be?
faq.answer: Each API call produces 5 seconds to 5 minutes of audio (music_length_ms: 5000-300000). For longer compositions, generate sections separately and stitch them together in audio editing software.

faq.question: Can I generate music without vocals?
faq.answer: Yes. Set force_instrumental: true in the API call and include 'no vocals' in your prompt description for belt-and-suspenders confirmation.

faq.question: What audio format does the API output?
faq.answer: Default output is MP3 standard (44.1kHz stereo). WAV format is also available - see the model page API documentation for the full format list and pricing per format.

faq.question: Can I use the generated music commercially?
faq.answer: Yes. ElevenLabs Music output is commercial-friendly. However, ensure you have rights to any lyrics you provide - the skill does not check lyric provenance.

faq.question: How do I cancel a long-running generation?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl-C to cancel the remote request before exit. The CLI polls request status and downloads the result upon completion.

faq.question: Why does this skill need external_commands and filesystem access?
faq.answer: The skill invokes the runcomfy CLI which communicates with the RunComfy Model API. It requires filesystem access to store your API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/ for authentication.
